author: kevaundray
content: The idea was to make it hardfork independent, so if 8025 is enabled then nodes could process the execution payload via proofs, but if not then nodes would do what they do now with an EL

author: kevaundray
content: Im on mobile, so I can’t find the dialogue, but implementations would have the conditional flag that you noted — the initial rollout would actually do both EL verification and proof verification to allow us to collect metrics and build good observability infrastructure for this

author: kevaundray
content: Found the thread on the latter point: https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1451216716062523507/1464778559313809538

author: deme1744
content:
Yeah, I understood that. Basically just logging/metrics to see if its result is in line with EL execution.
And then as next step a node can start actually basing it's validation on it.

author: deme1744
content: I was just pointing out that in current specs, there is no such conditional. And also, it is an `assert` at the moment, making the specs already in this "next step".

author: deme1744
content: No big deal for now, as long as it is clear that the intent is to have this fork independant. I wasn't fully sure however initially, purely on the specs, but did assume so.

author: kevaundray
content: Ah right, yeah I think this is a good point -- Tau also made a comment re this yesterday: https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1483418721367363594/1483419358159175742

author: deme1744
content: Ah, I didn't see that. But yes, I did see that Fork based PR that got added later on, for tests to pass.
